title: Untitled document
content:
1. Ground No. 2 – Addition of Rs. 15,67,684/- under Section 69A of the Act

a) That during preceding year, i.e., FY 2019-20 pertaining to AY 2020-21, assessee company availed loan of Rs. 15,00,000/- from M/s Arti Securities and Services Ltd. which was assessed as unexplained cash credit u/s 68 of the Act.

That during the year under consideration, assessee company repaid loan alongwith interest thereupon to M/s Arti Securities and Services Ltd.

Chart having details of transactions of assessee company with M/s Arti Securities and Services Ltd. is as under:
Date
Amount Received
Amount Repaid
Interest
TDS
Interest Paid
Balance
23-Aug-19
15,00,000

15,00,000
30-Sep-19

16,027

15,16,027
30-Sep-19

1,603

15,14,424
18-Oct-19

14,424
15,00,000
31-Dec-19

37,808

15,37,808
31-Dec-19

3,781

15,34,027
31-Mar-20

37,397

15,71,424
31-Mar-20

3,740

15,67,684
11-Sep-20

15,67,684

-

Ld. AO presumed that against bank payments to M/s Arti Securities and Services Ltd.; assessee company received cash which formed unexplained money in hands of assessee company assessable u/s 69A of the Act.

b) In this regard it is clarified that the loan availed by assessee company was a genuine liability and was repaid during the year under consideration.

Apart from mere presumption and theories, there do not exist any evidence suggesting that cash was received by assessee company and additions are made to income of assessee company only on suspicion which is not tenable under the law.

c) Without prejudice to the above, it is pertinent to mention here that the loan received by assessee company from M/s Arti Securities and Services Ltd. was assessed as unexplained cash credit during AY 2020-21, i.e., it was assumed that unexplained cash was utilized by assessee for availing said loan and thus, the impugned amount was assessed as its income.

Thereafter, at the time of repayment, it can only be assumed that the assessee has received back the unexplained cash paid earlier and thus, the cash assumed to be received by assessee is not assessable as income again during the year under consideration as it will amount to double taxation once at the time of utilization of the same for availing accommodation entry of loan and again at the time of regeneration of the same at the time of repayment of loan.

Discharge of onus

It is not out of place to mention here that the assessee company with a view to discharge the onus cast upon it has furnished following evidences to substantiate genuineness of transactions:
Evidences w.r.t. genuineness of loans from M/s Arti Securities and Services Ltd.

- Confirmation of account for FY 2019-20.
158
- Confirmation of account for FY 2020-21.
159
- Acknowledgement of ITR filed by M/s Arti Securities and Services Ltd.
160
- Bank statement of M/s Arti Securities and Services Ltd.
161-163

It is pertinent to mention here that from perusal of confirmation of account from M/s Arti Securities and Services Ltd. it is evident that said entities have duly confirmed the transaction with assessee company.

From perusal of chart and details enumerated supra the following is evident:

i. The loan in question whose addition was made by the Ld. AO to income of assessee company during AY 2020-21 by treating the same as unexplained has already been repaid by the appellant.
ii. Appellant company has paid principal amount as well as interest to the lenders
iii. As required u/s 194A of the Income tax Act, TDS has also been deducted by the appellant company on the payment of interest.
iv. Repayment of the loan has been done long before the issue of notice u/s 148 on 22.03.2024.
v. Loans were repaid on 11/09/2020, i.e., even before conduct of search in case of Galaxy Group (D.O.S. 17/11/2021)

That apart we are relying on latest judicial pronouncement of Hon’ble ITAT Delhi dated 27/03/2025 in the case of Real Innerspring Technologies Pvt. Ltd. vs. ACIT, ITA No. 647/Del/2023 wherein in similar circumstances additions made were deleted.

In view of the facts of the case and the judicial pronouncements cited and relied upon supra, it is requested that the addition of Rs. 15,67,684/- made by Ld. AO u/s 69A of the Act deserves to be deleted and may please be deleted.

2. Ground No. 3 – Estimated commission payment on alleged bogus loan.

a) That further addition of Rs. 47,030/- was made on account of alleged cash expenditure on commission paid for availing accommodation entry.

b) In this regard it is submitted that since genuineness of loan transactions is already substantiated vide submissions supra, the addition made u/s 69C of the Act shall also be deleted.

3. Ground No. 4 – Unsecured loans received from promoter/ directors assessed as unexplained cash credit u/s 68 of Act.

a) That during the year under consideration, assessee company has following transactions with its promoter/ directors:

Name of Party
Opening Balance
Amount Received
Amount Repaid
Interest
TDS
Closing Balance
1
Sh. Vineet Gupta
(PAN - AAIPG0049D)
Prop. Om Gems and Jewellery
11,75,41,880
9,14,88,733
7,85,83,000
68,58,680
5,14,401
13,67,91,892

As evident the assessee company had received a sum of Rs. 9,14,88,733/- from its promoter/ director during the year under consideration and said amount is assessed as unexplained cash credits u/s 68 of the Act.

b) During the course of assessment proceedings, assessee company furnished following evidences to substantiate identity and genuineness of transactions and creditworthiness of promoter/ directors:
Evidences w.r.t. genuineness of loans from Sh. Vineet Gupta (prop. M/s Om Gems and Jewellery)

- Confirmation of account
120-123
- Relevant extract of bank statement
124-146
- Acknowledgement of ITR filed by Sh. Vineet Gupta.
147
- Audited Financials
148-157

c) It is not out of place to mention here that there do not exist any adverse evidence regarding business activities of Sh. Vineet Gupta.

There is no evidence of introduction of any unaccounted funds in their bank accounts prior transfer of funds to assessee company.

The Ld. AO merely on surmises, presumptions and conjectures didn’t accept genuineness of transactions and creditworthiness of Sh. Vineet Gupta. In view of the stated facts of the case it is evident that the additions made by Ld. AO to the income of assessee company is not tenable under the law.

We are relying on following judicial pronouncements to substantiate that the assessee company has duly discharged the onus cast upon it and no additions shall be made to its income:
i. Addl. CIT, Special Range vs. Prayag Polytech Pvt. Ltd., ITA No. 5970/Del/2017
ii. D. J. Stock Broking Pvt. Ltd. vs. ITO, ITA No. 313/Ahd/2017, Order dt. 03/03/2020
iii. ITO vs. Computer Home Information Plus Pvt. Ltd., ITA No. 5680/Del/2016
iv. PCIT vs. Ami Industries (India) (P.) Ltd., (2020) 116 taxmann.com 34 (Bom)
v. Kumar Nirman & Nivesh (P) Ltd. vs. ACIT, (2020) 121 taxmann.com 174 (Kar)
vi. Cornerstone Property Investments (P) Ltd. vs. ITO, (2023) 152 taxmann.com 256 (Kar)(HC)
In view of facts of the case and judicial pronouncements cited and relied upon supra, additions made by Ld. AO u/s 68 of the Act is not tenable under the law and deserves to be deleted and may please be deleted.
Page 2 of 2

insights.summary: The document is an appeal against the addition of Rs. 15,67,684 to the income of the assessee company under Section 69A of the Act, and other additions related to unexplained cash credits and estimated commission payments.
keyDetails: Addition of Rs. 15,67,684 under Section 69A of the Act
Loan availed from M/s Arti Securities and Services Ltd.
Confirmation of account and bank statement from M/s Arti Securities and Services Ltd.
Addition of Rs. 47,030 for alleged cash expenditure on commission
Unsecured loans received from promoter/directors assessed as unexplained cash credit
mitigations: Providing confirmation of account and bank statement
Furnishing evidence of genuineness of transactions
obligations: Compliance with tax laws and regulations
recommendedActions: Delete the addition of Rs. 15,67,684 under Section 69A of the Act
Delete the addition of Rs. 47,030 for alleged cash expenditure on commission
Reconsider the assessment of unsecured loans received from promoter/directors

title: Untitled document
content:
Run the application

DocGenerator

Hand off

Commit

+3,658
-88

kill server run again

Worked for 26s
The server is restarted cleanly and the app is running at http://localhost:3000.

I verified http://localhost:3000/login is returning 200 OK.

add an option to even send documents, menaing i can upload a document and share it like how we share doc but that will also be password protected and the userto whom we send can download the document we sent preview it and attach a e signature to a new page generated and added to the uploaded document strictly document uploaded should be a pdf

Worked for 8m 23s
I added a full secure uploaded-PDF send flow.

What works now:

Upload a PDF from the workspace and create a password-protected share link.
Recipient can open the shared page, preview the uploaded PDF, and download it.
If signature is required, the recipient can e-sign, and docrud appends a new signature certificate page to the original uploaded PDF.
The signed PDF becomes the new downloadable preview for that shared document.
Main changes:

Workspace sender flow in components/DocumentGenerator.tsx
Shared recipient page in app/documents/[id]/page.tsx
Public document routes:
app/api/public/documents/[id]/route.ts
app/api/public/documents/[id]/sign/route.ts
app/api/public/documents/[id]/pdf/route.ts
PDF signature-page helper in lib/server/shared-uploaded-pdf.ts
Document model updates in types/document.ts and lib/server/history.ts
I also added pdf-lib to support appending the signature page.

Checks passed:

npm run lint
npm run build
Only the same old warning remains in components/BusinessSettingsCenter.tsx (line 281).

Your app can be tested at http://localhost:3000. In Generate Documents, use the new Send Secure PDF card.

9 files changed
+477
-20
Undo
app/api/documents/route.ts
app/api/public/documents/[id]/pdf/route.ts
app/api/public/documents/[id]/route.ts
app/api/public/documents/[id]/sign/route.ts
app/documents/[id]/page.tsx
components/DocumentGenerator.tsx
lib/server/history.ts
lib/server/shared-uploaded-pdf.ts
types/document.ts
kill server and run again

Worked for 25s
The server is restarted cleanly and the app is running at http://localhost:3000.

I verified http://localhost:3000/login is returning 200 OK.

the new signed page which you are adding add all terms and conditions of signing as well as make it legally strong according to indian laws, as well as add watermark which the super admin or in saas users can add for each document

Worked for 9m 23s
I updated the signing flow in three places:

The appended PDF signature page is now much stronger and more formal in shared-uploaded-pdf.ts. It records signer consent, timestamp, IP, location, audit/evidence notes, and India-aware execution cautions. I kept the wording legally careful: it supports attribution and recordkeeping, but it does not falsely claim that every signature is automatically a CCA-backed eSign or DSC.
Per-document watermarking is now wired into the workspace in DocumentGenerator.tsx. Admins and SaaS users can set a watermark label per document, and that label is carried into generated docs, uploaded PDF shares, and the final signed PDF.
Uploaded PDFs are now watermarked server-side in route.ts, and the same watermark is preserved when the signed certificate page is appended in /Users/kushagra/Desktop/DocGenerator/app/api/public/documents/[id]/sign/route.ts.
The app is running again at http://localhost:3000, and http://localhost:3000/login is returning 200 OK.

Checks:

npm run lint passed, with the same existing warning in BusinessSettingsCenter.tsx (line 281).
npm run build is still not clean because of pre-existing unrelated build issues around /_error, /login, and /workspace, including a next/document import problem and a missing chunk during prerender. I did not change those flows in this pass.
Legal basis I used for the certificate wording:

Information Technology Act, 2000
CCA eSign overview
Bharatiya Sakshya Adhiniyam, 2023
If you want, I can take the next pass on the remaining build errors so this whole flow is production-clean too.

4 files changed
+170
-16
Undo
app/api/history/route.ts
app/api/public/documents/[id]/sign/route.ts
components/DocumentGenerator.tsx
lib/server/shared-uploaded-pdf.ts
fix the page layout make it look more premium MNC grade professional and add a watermark of docrud also to the signature page which can be removed if user has purchased plan and for super admin keep it an option to add or not

Worked for 2m 57s
I upgraded the signed certificate page to feel much more premium and controlled.

What changed:

The appended signature page layout in shared-uploaded-pdf.ts now uses a stronger MNC-style structure:
dark executive header band
cleaner signing-event details card
better signature area hierarchy
more polished legal/evidentiary section
refined footer treatment
I added a dedicated docrud certificate watermark on the signature page itself.
That branding is now controllable per document through the workspace in DocumentGenerator.tsx.
Branding rules now:

Free/trial or restricted SaaS flows keep the docrud signature-page watermark enabled.
Paid SaaS users can turn it off per document.
Super admin can also toggle it on or off per document.
The setting is saved into document state via document.ts, respected during history creation in route.ts, and used when the recipient signs in /Users/kushagra/Desktop/DocGenerator/app/api/public/documents/[id]/sign/route.ts.
Checks:

npm run lint passed, with the same existing warning in BusinessSettingsCenter.tsx (line 281).
The app is running cleanly at http://localhost:3000.
http://localhost:3000/login is returning 200 OK.
Try the Send Secure PDF flow again and sign one test PDF. You should now see the upgraded certificate layout and the new certificate-branding toggle in the workspace.

4 files changed
+136
-13
Undo
app/api/public/documents/[id]/sign/route.ts
components/DocumentGenerator.tsx
lib/server/shared-uploaded-pdf.ts
types/document.ts
still signing page layout is not good text is overlapping make it proper adjust everything properly as well as imporve preview mode in mobile devices things overlapp make it proper

Worked for 4m 33s
I fixed both layout problems.

The signed certificate page in shared-uploaded-pdf.ts no longer relies on brittle fixed bottom coordinates. It now uses a top-to-bottom flow with:

header band
signing details card
signature block
legal notes block
automatic continuation onto another appended page if the legal text needs more space
That removes the overlap between the signature area and the legal text.

I also cleaned up the shared document page in /Users/kushagra/Desktop/DocGenerator/app/documents/[id]/page.tsx for mobile:

tighter title sizing
better button layout on phones
safer preview container sizing
less aggressive split layouts
full-width signing CTA on small screens
cleaner iframe heights so the preview doesn’t collide with surrounding sections
Checks:

npm run lint passed, with the same old warning in BusinessSettingsCenter.tsx (line 281)
http://localhost:3000/login is returning 200 OK
Please try the secure PDF signing flow again now, especially on mobile. The signature certificate should be much cleaner and the shared preview page should stop overlapping.

2 files changed
+224
-138
Undo
app/documents/[id]/page.tsx
lib/server/shared-uploaded-pdf.ts
add an AI expert "DoXpert" who is an intelligent document reader and advisor which reads documents and give summary with document score and sentiments as well as it can advice and suggestions what can be replied back or what can be added to the document for specific low score areas, warn the user if the document can harm the user in any sense, do proper risk mitigation of documents, make this robust and as a separate feature in side bar with full screen modern AI chatbot interface for both super admin and SAAS users create specific plans according to this new feature and update on frontend and backend both

I’m turning this into a real product surface instead of hiding it inside the parser: a dedicated DoXpert workspace with its own sidebar entry, full-screen advisor UI, document scoring/risk guidance, and SaaS feature gating. I’m checking the current AI routes, sidebar tabs, and plan definitions first so the new feature lands cleanly across both backend and frontend.

Explored 2 files, 1 search
I’m packaging DoXpert as its own workspace surface instead of bloating the generator tab. That lets me ship a cleaner full-screen AI reader/advisor, hook it into SaaS feature gating properly, and keep plan management/admin controls consistent.

Explored 2 files
Ran python3 - <<'PY' from pathlib import Path p=Path('/Users/kushagra/Desktop/DocGenerator/components/DocumentGenerator.tsx') for i,l in enumerate(p.read_text().splitlines(),1): if 'TabsContent value="dashboard"' in l or 'TabsContent value="summary"' in l or 'TabsContent value="history"' in l or 'TabsContent value="generate"' in l or 'setActiveTab(' in l and 'doxpert' in l: print(f'{i}: {l}') PY

I’m keeping DoXpert focused: one premium AI route, one dedicated full-screen workspace, and one new SaaS entitlement. That gives you a real product feature instead of another hidden tool card, while keeping the rollout manageable and coherent.

Explored 1 file
Ran python3 - <<'PY' from pathlib import Path p=Path('/Users/kushagra/Desktop/DocGenerator/components/DocumentGenerator.tsx') for i,l in enumerate(p.read_text().splitlines(),1): if 'TabsList' in l or 'TabsTrigger value=' in l or 'activeTab' in l and 'Tabs' in l: print(f'{i}: {l}') PY

Explored 1 file
I’ve got the main shape: DoXpert will be a premium entitlement beside AI Copilot, not a hidden parser. I’m now adding the feature key and plan coverage first so the new sidebar tab only appears where it should.

Explored 3 files
I’m putting DoXpert behind its own SaaS entitlement and making it available only on stronger paid plans by default. That keeps the feature commercially meaningful while still giving admin full access and a clean upgrade story in the UI.

insights.summary: Development of a secure document sharing and signing application with AI-powered document analysis and advisory features
keyDetails: Compliance with Indian laws and regulations
risks: Compliance risks with Indian laws and regulations
Dependence on third-party libraries and tools
Potential for errors in document analysis and advisory
mitigations: Regularly reviewing and updating compliance with Indian laws and regulations
Conducting thorough testing and quality assurance
Providing clear documentation and user support
obligations: Compliance with Indian laws and regulations
Maintenance of data security and privacy
Provision of accurate and reliable document analysis and advisory
recommendedActions: Conduct regular security audits and testing
Review and update compliance with Indian laws and regulations
Provide user training and support for document analysis and advisory features
